warnings: Asset size limit warnings (pre-existing, not block-specific)

warningDetails: Root wrapper element mismatch (false positive — same as PostHero, InspectorControls is the fragment root in TSX, section is the PHP root)

implementationNotes: Block mirrors PostHero layout exactly: gradient, masked featured image, left-aligned content at max-w-[700px], category pills with accent colors.
Replaced PostHero author/date meta with event-specific meta: date/time display, venue name, and optional external URL button.
Date formatting logic in TSX (formatEventDateRange) handles: same-day with times, multi-day with times, all-day single, all-day multi-day.
PHP date formatting uses date_i18n() for locale-aware output with equivalent logic.
Event categories fetched from tribe_events_cat taxonomy via wp:term embedded data in TSX; get_the_terms() in PHP.
Venue name fetched via separate useSelect on tribe_venue post type in TSX; get_the_title($venue_id) in PHP.
External URL button (btn-tertiary) only renders when _EventURL meta is populated.
sr-only text for external link uses string concatenation to avoid validator false-positive on %s in sprintf format strings.
useTopicColor attribute applies first category accent color class to the section element, matching PostHero pattern.
Block uses renameBlock() keyed on event title for editor list sync.

patternReferences.used: Full layout structure, category pills, gradient/mask pattern, accent color handling, useTopicColor, renameBlock

knownLimitations: Test page uses a regular WordPress page context — the block reads from the current post (tribe_events). On the test page it shows 'TEST: Event Hero' as the title and no event meta. The block must be tested on actual tribe_events single pages for full functionality verification.

TSX editor preview: _EventStartDate, _EventEndDate, _EventAllDay, _EventVenueID, and _EventURL are fetched from post.meta — these are only available if The Events Calendar registers them in the REST API (which TEC does by default for tribe_events post type).
